title: Eval / Canary
description: ML / product engineer view: canary eval run history — correctness, judge scores, hallucination, retrieval, cost, and regressions vs. the committed baseline (canary_runs / canary_run_results, persisted by src.eval.run_agent --persist-db).

list.name: qid
list.label: Question (per-question history)
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT r.qid FROM canary_run_results r JOIN canary_runs cr ON cr.id = r.canary_run_id WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic' AND r.qid IS NOT NULL ORDER BY r.qid;

panels.title: Correctness Accuracy (overall + by kind)
panels.description: correctness_overall plus per-kind accuracy from correctness->'by_kind', run_kind='agentic'.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, 'overall' AS metric, correctness_overall AS value FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) UNION ALL SELECT cr.created_at AS time, kind_stats.kind || '.acc' AS metric, (kind_stats.value->>'acc')::double precision AS value FROM canary_runs cr, jsonb_each(cr.correctness->'by_kind') AS kind_stats(kind, value) WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(cr.created_at) ORDER BY 1;

SELECT t AS time, 'baseline (overall)' AS metric, correctness_overall AS value FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Judge — Faithfulness (mean)
panels.description: faithfulness_mean per agentic run vs. baseline. Does the answer stay grounded in retrieved context?

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, (judge->>'faithfulness_mean')::double precision AS faithfulness FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

SELECT t AS time, (judge->>'faithfulness_mean')::double precision AS baseline FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Judge — Relevance (mean)
panels.description: relevance_mean per agentic run vs. baseline. Does the answer address the question?

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, (judge->>'relevance_mean')::double precision AS relevance FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

SELECT t AS time, (judge->>'relevance_mean')::double precision AS baseline FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Judge — Citation Accuracy (mean)
panels.description: citation_accuracy_mean per agentic run vs. baseline. Do the cited refs actually support the claims?

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, (judge->>'citation_accuracy_mean')::double precision AS citation_accuracy FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

SELECT t AS time, (judge->>'citation_accuracy_mean')::double precision AS baseline FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Judge — Completeness (mean)
panels.description: completeness_mean per agentic run vs. baseline. Does the answer cover the full expected content?

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, (judge->>'completeness_mean')::double precision AS completeness FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

SELECT t AS time, (judge->>'completeness_mean')::double precision AS baseline FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;
SELECT created_at AS time, hallucination_rate_mean FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

SELECT t AS time, hallucination_rate_mean AS baseline FROM canary_runs CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS bounds(t) WHERE run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Retrieval recall@k / precision@k
panels.description: recall@5/10 and precision@5/10 from the retrieval jsonb column, run_kind='agentic'.

targets.rawSql: SELECT cr.created_at AS time, m.key AS metric, (m.value)::double precision AS value FROM canary_runs cr, jsonb_each_text(cr.retrieval) AS m(key, value) WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic' AND m.key IN ('recall@5','recall@10','precision@5','precision@10') AND $__timeFilter(cr.created_at) ORDER BY 1;

SELECT b.t AS time, 'baseline ' || m.key AS metric, (m.value)::double precision AS value FROM canary_runs cr, jsonb_each_text(cr.retrieval) AS m(key, value) CROSS JOIN (VALUES ($__timeFrom()::timestamptz), ($__timeTo()::timestamptz)) AS b(t) WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic_baseline' AND m.key IN ('recall@5','recall@10','precision@5','precision@10') ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Latest Run Regressions
panels.description: Per-question regressions flagged vs. the committed baseline on the most recent agentic run. 0 = green, >0 = red.

targets.rawSql: SELECT jsonb_array_length(regressions) AS regression_count FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;

panels.title: Total Cost per Run (USD)
panels.description: total_cost_usd per agentic run — watch for cost regressions from model/prompt/agent-loop changes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, total_cost_usd FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

panels.title: Total Latency per Run (s)
panels.description: total_latency_s per agentic run — summed per-question latency.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, total_latency_s FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY created_at;

panels.title: Recent Runs
panels.description: Last 20 runs (includes the backfilled agentic_baseline row for reference — see run_kind).

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at, run_kind, run_description, git_sha, model, reasoning_effort, judge_model, evaluated, total_questions, correctness_overall, hallucination_rate_mean, total_cost_usd, jsonb_array_length(regressions) AS regression_count FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind IN ('agentic', 'agentic_baseline') ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;

panels.title: Lowest-Scoring / Regressed Questions (latest run)
panels.description: Per-question detail for the most recent agentic run, worst-first (incorrect + high hallucination first).

targets.rawSql: SELECT r.qid, r.question, r.kind, r.route, r.correct, (r.judge->'faithfulness'->>'score')::int AS faithfulness, (r.judge->'citation_accuracy'->>'score')::int AS citation_accuracy, r.hallucination_rate, r.latency_s, r.cost_usd FROM canary_run_results r WHERE r.canary_run_id = (SELECT id FROM canary_runs WHERE run_kind = 'agentic' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) ORDER BY r.correct ASC NULLS FIRST, r.hallucination_rate DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 25;

panels.title: Per-Question History — $qid
panels.description: Selected question's correctness (0/1) and judge faithfulness score across all agentic runs. The Grafana-native replacement for diffing two run JSONs by hand.

targets.rawSql: SELECT cr.created_at AS time, 'correct' AS metric, (r.correct::int)::double precision AS value FROM canary_run_results r JOIN canary_runs cr ON cr.id = r.canary_run_id WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic' AND r.qid = '$qid' AND $__timeFilter(cr.created_at) UNION ALL SELECT cr.created_at AS time, 'faithfulness/5' AS metric, ((r.judge->'faithfulness'->>'score')::double precision / 5.0) AS value FROM canary_run_results r JOIN canary_runs cr ON cr.id = r.canary_run_id WHERE cr.run_kind = 'agentic' AND r.qid = '$qid' AND $__timeFilter(cr.created_at) ORDER BY 1;
